name: Chicken Pot Pie#2
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup cut fresh or frozen green beans, cut into 1-inch pieces
1/2 cup fresh or frozen whole kernel corn
1/2 cup fresh or frozen English peas or butter beans
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first three ingredients in a large Dutch oven; bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat, and simmer until tender. Drain chicken, reserving 2 cups broth. Skim fat from broth.
3. Set chicken and broth aside to cool slightly. Cook potato and carrot in boiling water to cover for 5 minutes. Add green beans; cook for 3 minutes. Drain.
4. Skin and bone the chicken; cut into bite-size pieces. Set aside.
5. Melt butter in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion, and cook, stirring constantly, until tender.
6. Add 1/4 cup flour, stirring until smooth. Cook, stirring constantly, until mixture is light golden.
7. Gradually add reserved broth. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until mixture thickens and is bubbly. Remove from heat.
8. Stir in chicken, cooked vegetables, corn, and the next five ingredients. Pour into a lightly greased 11- x 7- x 11/2-inch baking dish; set aside.
9. Combine 1 1/2 cups flour, baking powder, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a bowl. Add sour cream, stirring with a fork until dry ingredients are moistened.
10. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface; knead 3 or 4 times. Press dough to 1/4-inch thickness. Cut with a 21/2-inch biscuit cutter.
11. Arrange biscuits over pie.
12. Bake at 400 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes, or until golden. Serve immediately.
